one_time_description: Settlement Proceeds - First Nations Drinking Water ($500,000) and Yorkton Tribal Council Child and Family Services - Canadian Human Rights Tribunal Settlement ($1,548,650)
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in multiple limited partnerships including Birds Point Resort, Weyotisiwin Holdings, Kahkewistahaw Business Landing, Mamawi Holdings, Gas & Convenience Store, Penipa Hotel. 14.28% investment in PHC Holdings Limited Partnership. Flood claim and treaty benefit claim against Government of Canada ongoing. Kihew Fab-Co Ltd. (50% owned) has sustainability concerns with full loan allowance recorded.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 - this is a 2023 statement not 2024. Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). Two entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs): Kahkewistahaw Development LP and Kahkewistahaw Management LP. Several entities excluded from consolidation as not controlled by Chief and Council (Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust - Trust Account, Community Development Account, Authorized Expense Account, Legacy Trust). Consolidated entities include 50% proportionate consolidation of Kihew Fab-Co Ltd. and Ochapowace/Kahkewistahaw Home Care. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($16,686,222) + CMHC ($2,346,540) + YTC CHRT Settlement ($1,548,650) + ISC FNIHB ($55,487) = $20,636,899 approximately. Ongoing flood claim and treaty benefit claim against Canada. Loans taken to fund claims with insurance coverage. Large one-time items include CHRT settlement and drinking water settlement.
